daily_take.headline: Peru's kingmaker just flipped. Fujimori's surge signals backroom deal or market euphoria.
daily_take.deck: Keiko Fujimori rocketed 23.5 points to 57.5% in 24 hours while her main rival López Aliaga cratered 32 points. That's not organic. Either fresh polling moved the needle or traders are front-running a coalition announcement. The math doesn't work if this is about new data alone, which means smart money might be arbitraging off fractured information. Short López Aliaga further or wait for Fujimori to overcorrect past 65%.
